expected_effect: Magnify the low-retail and low-volatility gains from screen_0002 without reopening broad cheap mode or arb leakage.
expected_failure_signature: If mean fees stay elevated without another step-up in low-retail and low-volatility slices, the floor is still too sticky; if arb leakage rises, the skim is too permissive.
expected_win_condition: Increase mean_edge materially over screen_0002 by letting the non-toxic side win more calm flow while the toxic side remains explicitly protected.
hidden_coupling_removed: The first split still keeps too much hazard spend in the shared floor, muting the asymmetry advantage of the toxic-side overlay.
rationale: Exploit screen_0002 by shifting more hazard spend out of the symmetric floor into the toxic-side overlay and allowing only a tightly capped non-toxic skim of baseline flex under calm confirmations.
title: Baseline split with capped floor skim
why_not_coefficient_retune: This keeps the two-channel assembly but changes which channel owns hazard spend and how much baseline flex the non-toxic side may reclaim.
